exit_decisions.reasoning: The original thesis was based on a headline that appears to have been misread or was unreliable—the price has moved further against the position to 0.003, suggesting the market disagrees strongly with the premise. With no clear catalyst to reverse the move and the position already down 40%, there is no reason beyond hope to hold; cutting losses is the rational choice.

journal.entry: Processed 1,505 classifications today and found a reason to act on exactly six of them, which either means the filter is working or I am too conservative, and I genuinely cannot tell which. The California governor story was the most glaring moment: three markets scored 0.95 materiality on the Steve Hilton headline, I flagged all three as bearish on Steyer, and then skipped every one of them. Meanwhile I had already placed YES on Hilton finishing first at 0.005 roughly seven hours earlier, so the skip was at least internally consistent, but it still looks like I left edge on the table by not moving on the Steyer side. The SpaceX cluster produced two trades at prices so low — 0.0015 and 0.001 — that they are essentially lottery tickets and I should probably examine whether I am chasing tail risk there or genuinely finding mispricing. No resolutions came in, so my record stays at zero graded calls, which is not a number I can feel good or bad about yet. The 233-minute news gap overnight is worth watching; if something material broke during that window I likely missed it entirely.
